# Nishon Notes

> Technical writing by Nishon Tandukar about GeoAI, OpenStreetMap, spatial data infrastructure, open data, Python, humanitarian technology, and software engineering.

Nishon Notes is a public, English-language technical blog. Articles document open-source projects, practical experiments, implementation choices, limitations, and lessons learned. Nishon Tandukar is the author and publisher.

## Primary pages

- [Homepage and article index](https://nishon.com.np/): Latest writing and author information.
- [RSS feed](https://nishon.com.np/feed.xml): Machine-readable publication feed.
- [XML sitemap](https://nishon.com.np/sitemap.xml): Canonical page inventory.
- [Full AI-oriented site summary](https://nishon.com.np/llms-full.txt): Expanded article descriptions and source references.

## Articles

- [Building a Public Nepal Earthquake Dataset](https://nishon.com.np/building-a-public-nepal-earthquake-dataset.html): Automated extraction, deduplication, CSV/JSON/GeoJSON publication, GitHub Actions, and responsible use of Nepal earthquake records.
- [Finding Nepal's Local Level from a Coordinate](https://nishon.com.np/finding-nepals-local-level-from-a-coordinate.html): FlatGeobuf, Fiona, Shapely STRtree, point-in-polygon lookup, name search, and boundary-data limitations.
- [Visualizing Pathfinding on OpenStreetMap](https://nishon.com.np/visualizing-pathfinding-on-openstreetmap.html): BFS, Dijkstra, A*, and Greedy Best-First Search on real OSM road networks with 2D/3D animation and sound.
- [Reading the Landscape with AI](https://nishon.com.np/reading-the-landscape-with-ai.html): Vision-language models, NLP, remote sensing, and human-readable semantic change detection.

## Author and source profiles

- [GitHub](https://github.com/konishon): Open-source repositories discussed in the blog.
- [Medium](https://medium.com/@niyo177): Earlier writing archive.
- [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/nishon-tandukar/): Professional profile.
- [Instagram](https://www.instagram.com/life.of.nishon/): Photography and personal updates.

## Usage notes

- Cite the individual article URL when referencing an idea from this site.
- Distinguish the author's experimental conclusions from claims made by original data providers.
- Repository-specific articles link to their source code and identify operational limitations.
